commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=9e12298d0051e1a4fabae67596b15b673a091483)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a `gateway/chat-abort` subsystem logger, logs rejected terminal persistence in `registerChatAbortController` cleanup, and adds a Vitest case for that rejection branch.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +39. Total +44 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection on current `main` shows rejected `projectSessionTerminalPersistence` promises can reach catch handlers that clean up or project fallback state without logging; I did not execute a failing runtime repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs contributor-provided real behavior proof and a targeted code update for the delayed tracking path, so it is not a ClawSweeper repair/automerge candidate from this review.

target.title: fix(gateway): log errors when terminal persistence rejects in chat-abort cleanup
target.body:
Related to #97795.

## What Problem This Solves

`projectSessionTerminalPersistence` rejections in `registerChatAbortController` cleanup are silently swallowed — no log, no telemetry. Operators cannot observe session-store write failures in production, losing visibility into database or filesystem health.

## Why This Change Was Made

Add `log.error` at the exact rejection site (the `.catch()` in `cleanup`) before deleting the abort controller. This is the caller boundary — the cleanup closure owns both the abort controller lifecycle and the terminal persistence promise. Fixing here gives the most precise failure context (`runId` + error message) without changing any downstream persistence logic.

A module-level `createSubsystemLogger("gateway/chat-abort")` follows the established gateway pattern (used by `server-close.ts`, `sessions-history-http.ts`, etc.) rather than threading a `log` parameter through `registerChatAbortController` — keeping the diff minimal.

## Root Cause

`introduced by` commit `730c7269ef` (Vincent Koc, 2026-06-20). The original cleanup implementation omitted logging in the `.catch()` branch.

Confidence: **clear** — the empty `() => {}` catch handler at the affected lines visibly omits any error recording.

## User Impact

None — this is an observability fix for operators.

## Evidence

**Behavior addressed**: When `projectSessionTerminalPersistence` rejects, the gateway logs the failure before cleaning up the abort controller entry. The log message includes the `runId` and the original error text.

**Real environment tested**: Linux (kernel 4.19), Node v22, branch `fix/chat-abort-persistence-log-97795` (HEAD `9e12298d00`), based on origin/main `68ead4dd80`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**:

```console
$ node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/gateway/chat-abort.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts
EXIT=0

$ npx oxfmt --check src/gateway/chat-abort.ts src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts
All matched files use the correct format.

$ npx vitest run src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts --reporter=verbose
 ✓ |gateway-client| registerChatAbortController > logs errors when terminal persistence rejects 3ms
```

**Evidence after fix**:

| Input pattern | Result |
|---|---|
| `ProjectSessionTerminalPersistence` resolves | cleanup deletes entry after `.then()` (existing) |
| `ProjectSessionTerminalPersistence` rejects | **NEW**: `log.error` called → then cleanup deletes entry |
| No terminal persistence set | cleanup deletes entry immediately (existing) |

```console
$ npx vitest run src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 ✓ |gateway-server|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > isChatStopCommandText > ...
 ✓ |gateway-server|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > registerChatAbortController > ...
 ✓ |gateway-server|  ... (63 server shard tests) ...
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > isChatStopCommandText > ...
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > registerChatAbortController > ...
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > registerChatAbortController > 
                      retains completed registrations until terminal persistence succeeds  1ms
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > registerChatAbortController > 
                      logs errors when terminal persistence rejects  3ms
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > registerChatAbortController > ...
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > abortChatRunById > ...
 ✓ |gateway-client|  ../../src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts > resolveInFlightRunSnapshot > ...

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  96 passed (96)
   Start at  22:37:33
   Duration  12.21s (transform 9.43s, setup 2.85s, import 7.66s, tests 930ms, environment 0ms)
```

**Observed result after fix**: The new regression test `logs errors when terminal persistence rejects` passes — it verifies that when `projectSessionTerminalPersistence` rejects with `new Error("disk full")`, the `mockLogError` spy receives a message containing `"Failed to persist chat abort terminal state for run run-persist-fail"` and the abort controller entry is still cleaned up. All 96 existing tests continue to pass (0 regressions).

**What was not tested**:
- Live gateway with a forced session-store write failure — this class of fix (add logging to an empty catch block) is source-verified; the ClawSweeper Codex review on June 29 confirmed "reproducible from source"
- `server-runtime-subscriptions.ts:102-105` — the `.catch(() => undefined)` at that site has a different semantic (preventing unhandled rejection + async cleanup chaining) and is out of scope for this PR

## Regression Test Plan

```console
$ npx vitest run src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts --reporter=verbose

 Test Files  3 passed (3)
      Tests  96 passed (96)
```

New test case `logs errors when terminal persistence rejects`:

1. Register an abort controller with `registerChatAbortController`
2. Set `projectSessionTerminalPersistence` to a rejected promise (`Promise.reject(new Error("disk full"))`)
3. Call `cleanup()` and flush microtasks
4. Assert `mockLogError` was called with a message containing the runId
5. Assert the controller entry was cleaned up

## Changes

- `src/gateway/chat-abort.ts`: +4 lines — add `createSubsystemLogger` import, module-level logger, and `log.error` in the `.catch()` handler
- `src/gateway/chat-abort.test.ts`: +38 lines — add `vi.mock` for the logging subsystem and a regression test for the rejection path
